面向组织和个人的开源密码管理器Bitwarden今天宣布，备受期待的单点登录(SSO)功能将于2020年9月推出。
带SSO的Bitwarden登录功能将密码安全与基于身份的单点登录集成在一起。现在，用户可以通过其现有身份提供商进行身份验证，并获得访问所有Bitwarden密码管理功能的权限。此安全功能还为管理员和最终用户带来了新的优势：
Bitwarden Login with SSO通过与任何使用SAML 2.0或OpenID Connect的现有SSO解决方案集成，为组织提供了身份管理的终极灵活性。
新功能将包含在更新的企业计划中。随着这个即将发布的版本，Bitwarden正在更新订阅计划，以便为所有用户提供更多功能和价值。有关计划功能和定价的更多信息，请阅读此处。
单点登录(SSO)是访问SaaS应用程序的用户身份验证服务。通过SSO，员工可以使用一组凭据(如用户名和密码)安全地访问多个应用程序。要使用SSO系统，应用程序必须支持SSO集成协议。
虽然SSO支持在企业SaaS应用程序中很常见，但许多在线服务不支持SSO。要安全地访问这些在线服务，员工必须设计自己的方法来确保密码安全，这会造成潜在的威胁和隐藏的漏洞。密码管理解决方案缩小了这一差距。
使用Bitwarden Login with SSO，企业可以应用其现有的单点登录安全控制来访问Bitwarden Vault中基于密码的应用程序，因此，员工可以获得针对其所有在线体验的端到端密码保护。
Bitwarden Login with SSO为员工高效、安全地在线操作提供了企业所需的安全基础。
与其他没有SSO集成或强制企业使用专有SSO服务和身份解决方案的密码管理器不同，Bitwarden允许公司将密码管理与现有的、基于标准的身份访问解决方案统一起来。
这种集成提供了两全其美的效果，因为企业可以方便地扩展其身份管理，同时将密码管理部署到每个人。
虽然使用SSO登录是使用Bitwarden的一个关键原因，但还有许多其他显著的优点。
开源：Bitwarden采用开源方法来增强用户信任，提供透明度和简单的检查来验证代码质量。开源软件也为充满活力和参与度的Bitwarden社区奠定了基础。
自托管选项：特定行业和公司需要自托管解决方案来实现安全性或合规性。Bitwarden提供内部部署或私有云中的自托管服务。
跨平台功能：Bitwarden支持所有主要浏览器、操作系统和移动设备，以及面向开发人员的命令行界面和强大的API。
完整的云产品：企业用户可以将Bitwarden实施为云服务，包括使用SSO登录功能，并确保所有数据都使用端到端加密进行保护。
要了解有关Bitwarden的更多信息，或者开始使用组织或个人帐户，请访问bitwarden.com。
note_en:
Bitwarden, the open source password manager for organizations and individuals, today announced the highly anticipated Login with Single Sign-On (SSO) capability will be available in September 2020.
 The Bitwarden Login with SSO feature integrates password security with identity-based single sign-on. Now users can authenticate through their existing identity provider and gain access to all Bitwarden password management capabilities. This security feature also introduces new advantages for administrators and end-users:
  Bitwarden Login with SSO gives organizations the ultimate flexibility for identity management by integrating with any existing SSO solution that uses SAML 2.0 or OpenID Connect.
 The new feature will be included on an updated Enterprise Plan. With this coming release, Bitwarden is updating subscription plans to incorporate more functionality and value for all users. For more information about Plan features and pricing, read  here.
  Single sign-on (SSO) is a user authentication service to access SaaS applications. With SSO, employees are able to use one set of credentials, such as a username and password, to securely access multiple applications. To work with SSO systems, applications must support an SSO integration protocol.
 While SSO support is common for enterprise SaaS applications, many online services do not support SSO. To access these online services securely, employees must devise their own methods to keep passwords safe, creating potential threats and hidden vulnerabilities. Password management solutions close this gap.
 With Bitwarden Login with SSO, enterprises apply their existing single sign-on security controls to access password-based applications in the Bitwarden Vault, and as a result, employees gain end-to-end password protection for all of their online experiences.
  Bitwarden Login with SSO delivers the secure foundation enterprises need for employees to operate efficiently and safely online.
 Unlike other password managers that have no SSO integration, or that force businesses to use proprietary SSO services and identity solutions, Bitwarden allows companies to unite password management with existing, standards-based identity access solutions.
 This integration delivers the best of both worlds as enterprises conveniently extend their identity management while deploying password management to everyone.
 While login with SSO is a key reason to use Bitwarden, there are many other notable advantages.
 Open-source: Bitwarden takes an open source approach to enhance user trust, offering transparency and easy scrutiny to validate code quality. Open source software also sets the foundation for a vibrant and engaged Bitwarden community.
  Self-host option: Select industries and companies need self-hosted solutions for security or compliance. Bitwarden offers self-hosting on-premises or in a private cloud.
  Cross-platform functionality: Bitwarden supports all major browsers, operating systems, and mobile devices, along with a command line interface and robust API for developers.
  Complete Cloud offering: Enterprise users can implement Bitwarden as a cloud service, including the Login with SSO feature, with the assurance that all data is secured with end-to-end encryption.
 To learn more about Bitwarden, or to get started with an organization or individual account, visit  bitwarden.com.

以印度为例。在当地的移动市场，两轮和三轮汽车开始成为迅速扩大的中产阶级的热门选择，他们正在寻找更负担得起的选择。电动汽车版本很受欢迎，因为与汽油替代品相比，它们的维护成本更低，可靠性更高。
只有一个问题，这也是每个试图从汽油转换为电动的国家都面临的问题：如何建设充电站网络，使这些车辆可以在车库以外的小范围内使用？
这是典型的先有鸡还是先有蛋的问题。你需要电动汽车才能在充电站赚钱，但在电动汽车普及之前，你负担不起建造充电站的费用。一些初创公司试图首先自己建设这些网络。也许最著名的例子是Better Place，这是一家以色列初创公司，在2013年死于负现金流之前，筹集了8亿美元的风险投资。特斯拉试图通过创建一个超级增压器网络来解决这个问题，既是鸡又是蛋。
这就是Statiq如此有趣的原因。该公司总部位于新德里郊区古鲁格拉姆(Gurugram)，正在使用多收入模式启动一个电动汽车充电网络，希望这将使其能够避免其他充电网络所面临的财务挑战。它在当前的Y Combinator批次中，并将在本月晚些时候的演示日上展示。
该公司的联合创始人阿克利特·班萨尔和拉格哈夫·阿罗拉之前曾合作担任顾问，并创建了一家在线购买照片的公司，最终月度活跃人数达到5万人。他们决定转向电动汽车，特别是充电设备。
“我们觉得有必要对气候做些什么，因为我们住在德里，德里是世界上污染最严重的城市之一，而印度是世界上很多污染城市的家园。所以我们想为此做点什么。“班萨尔说。当他们研究污染的原因时，他们了解到汽车尾气是当地问题的一大部分。他们寻找了替代方案，但电动汽车充电站在全国范围内基本上仍然不存在。
因此，他们于2019年10月成立了Statiq，并于今年5月正式推出。他们已经在德里、班加罗尔、孟买和周边地区安装了150多个充电站。
不过，让我们来谈谈经济问题，因为对我来说，这是他们故事中最吸引人的部分。正如我所说，Statiq有一种多收入模式。首先，终端用户向Statiq购买使用网络的订阅，然后用户支付每个收费会话的费用。这笔会话费在Statiq和物业所有者之间平分，给安装车站的房东带来了额外的收入增长。
当谈到安装时，Statiq有几个诀窍。首先，根据Bansal的说法，该公司的充电设备的成本大约是美国设备同等成本的三分之一。这使得获得基础技术的成本更低。在那里，该公司与房东谈判安装事宜，房东将支付固定的安装费用，以换取持续的时段费用。
最重要的是，充电站上有广告，提供了另一种收入来源，特别是在购物中心等知名度高的地点，这对成功的电动汽车充电网络至关重要。
简而言之，Statiq不必花费资金来安装充电设备-而且在今年早些时候向YC申请之前，他们已经能够自举了。Bansal表示，在加入他们的YC批次之前，该公司在其平台上有数十个充电站和数千个付费会话，“我们现在每周增长20%。”
下一个是什么？这一切都是关于刻意的缩放。印度的电动汽车市场正在启动，Statiq希望成为那些汽车所在的地方。班萨尔和他的联合创始人希望乘势而上，继续建设关键的基础设施。印度政府可能会继续提供帮助：它批准了数十亿美元的电动汽车和充电站激励措施，将经济进一步推向清洁汽车的未来。
note_en:
Take India for instance. In the local mobility market,  two- and three-wheel vehicles are starting to emerge as a popular option for a rapidly expanding middle class looking for more affordable options. EV versions are popular thanks to their reduced maintenance costs and higher reliability compared to gasoline alternatives.
   There’s just one problem, and it’s the same one faced by every country which has attempted to convert from gasoline to electric: how do you build out the charging station network to make these vehicles usable outside a small range from their garage?
 It’s the classic chicken-and-egg problem. You need EVs in order to make money on charging stations, but you can’t afford to build charging stations until EVs are popular. Some startups have attempted to build out these networks themselves first. Perhaps the most famous example was Better Place,  an Israeli startup that raised $800 million in venture capital before dying from negative cash flow back in 2013. Tesla has attempted to solve the problem by being both the chicken and egg by creating a network of  Superchargers.
 That’s what makes  Statiq so interesting. The company, based in the New Delhi suburb of Gurugram, is bootstrapping an EV charging network using a multi-revenue model that it hopes will allow it to avoid the financial challenges that other charging networks have faced. It’s in the current Y Combinator batch and will be presenting at Demo Day later this month.
 Akshit Bansal and Raghav Arora, the company’s co-founders, worked together previously as consultants and built a company for buying photos online, eventually reaching 50,000 monthly actives. They decided to make a pivot — a hard pivot really — into EVs and specifically charging equipment.
  “We felt the need to do something about the climate because we were living in Delhi and Delhi is one of the most polluted cities in the world, and India is home to a lot of the polluted cities in the world. So we wanted to do something about it,” Bansal said. As they researched the causes of pollution, they learned that automobile exhaust represented a large part of the problem locally. They looked at alternatives, but EV charging stations remain basically non-existent across the country.
 Thus, they founded Statiq in October 2019 and officially launched this past May. They have installed more than 150 charging stations in Delhi, Bangalore, and Mumbai and the surrounding environs.
 Let’s get to the economics though, since that to me is the most fascinating part of their story. Statiq as I noted has a multi-revenue model. First, end users buy a subscription from Statiq to use the network, and then users pay a fee per charging session. That session fee is split between Statiq and the property owner, giving landlords who install the stations an incremental revenue boost.
  When it comes to installation, Statiq has a couple of tricks up its sleeves. First, the company’s charging equipment — according to Bansal — costs roughly a third of the equivalent cost of U.S. equipment. That makes the base technology cheaper to acquire. From there, the company negotiates installations with landlords where the landlords will pay the fixed costs of installation in exchange for that continuing session charge fee.
 On top of all that, the charging stations have advertising on them, offering another income stream particularly in high-visibility locations like shopping malls which are critical for a successful EV charging network.
 In short, Statiq hasn’t had to outlay capital in order to put in place their charging equipment — and they were able to bootstrap before applying to YC earlier this year. Bansal said the company had dozens of charging stations and thousands of paid sessions on its platform before joining their YC batch, and “we are now growing 20% week-over-week.”
 What’s next? It’s all about deliberate scaling. The EV market is turning on in India, and Statiq wants to be where those cars are. Bansal and his co-founder are hoping to ride the wave, continuing to build out critical infrastructure along the way. India’s government will likely continue to help:  its approved billions of dollars in incentives for EVs and for charging stations, tipping the economics even further in the direction of a clean car future.
title: YC支持的Statiq希望引导印度的电动汽车充电网络
title_en: YC-backed Statiq wants to bootstrap India’s EV charging network

note:
虽然近年来诊断癌症和心脏问题的能力取得了长足的进步，但一个多世纪以来，检测抑郁症的方法一直顽固地保持不变：观察患者，询问他们的情况。
加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)发起了一项由苹果公司赞助并与其合作的重大新研究，旨在帮助彻底改变抑郁症的检测和治疗。
这项为期三年的研究本周开始，由加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)和苹果公司(Apple)的研究人员共同设计，目的是获得睡眠、体力活动、心率和日常生活等因素的客观衡量标准，以阐明这些因素与抑郁和焦虑症状之间的关系。
这项研究将利用苹果的技术，包括iPhone，Apple Watch和Beddit睡眠监测设备。将可量化的数据与焦虑和抑郁的症状联系起来，可以使医疗保健提供者注意到警告信号，防止抑郁发作，跟踪治疗的有效性，并确定抑郁的原因。
加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)校长吉恩·布洛克(Gene Block)说：“作为一名接受过睡眠专业培训的神经科学家，我对这次合作感到难以置信的兴奋，并希望这将导致心理健康研究取得重大进展。”
加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)抑郁大挑战赛(UCLA Depression Grand Challenges)主任、杰出的精神病学教授纳尔逊·弗莱默(Nelson Fremer)博士是这项研究的首席研究员。
弗莱默说：“这种合作利用了加州大学洛杉矶分校的深厚研究专长和苹果的创新技术，有可能改变行为健康研究和临床护理。”“目前治疗抑郁症的方法几乎完全依赖于抑郁症患者的主观回忆。这是获得客观和精确的测量以指导诊断和治疗的重要一步。“。
这项研究是抑郁症大挑战的最新里程碑，这是加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)一项雄心勃勃的倡议，由来自不同学科的研究人员参与，目的是识别导致抑郁症的遗传和环境因素，了解抑郁症在大脑和身体中造成的生物变化，加快诊断和治疗的进展，并结束与这种疾病相关的耻辱。加州大学洛杉矶分校之所以选择接受这一挑战，是因为抑郁症困扰着全球超过3亿人，每年导致近100万人自杀。
这项研究的试点阶段，从加州大学洛杉矶分校的健康患者中招募了150名参与者，本周开始。主要阶段将从2021年到2023年进行，将有大约3000名参与者参加，他们来自加州大学洛杉矶分校的健康患者和加州大学洛杉矶分校的学生团体。
参与者需要在他们的个人iPhone上下载一个研究应用程序。他们将收到一台Apple Watch和Beddit睡眠监视器，他们将在研究期间使用。参与者将通过定期的临床访谈和问卷，以及从电话、手表和睡眠监视器获得的数据来分享相关信息。
弗莱默强调，确保研究参与者数据的隐私和安全是加州大学洛杉矶分校和苹果公司的高度优先事项。加州大学洛杉矶分校将在一个安全的环境中处理和维护研究数据，只有加州大学洛杉矶分校研究团队的成员才能访问。加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)和苹果公司(Apple)只有在对数据进行编码并剥离姓名和其他联系信息后，才会对数据进行分析。
这项研究是在新冠肺炎大流行扰乱了人们的生活，促使人们关注焦虑和抑郁的时候进行的，而且身体距离的要求给科学研究带来了挑战。
“加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)和苹果公司(Apple)设计了这项研究，以便参与的所有方面都可以远程完成，”弗莱默说。“这场流行病在全球范围内加剧了焦虑和抑郁，并提高了人们对行为健康对整体福祉重要性的认识。与此同时，身体距离的要求限制了面对面的精神健康评估和治疗，导致远程医疗的广泛使用和接受。这些变化突显了将这项研究中将要测试的技术纳入临床研究并最终付诸实践的重要性。“。
加州大学洛杉矶分校抑郁症大挑战赛在理解和治疗抑郁症方面已经取得了重大进展。乔纳森·弗林特(Jonathan Flint)教授领导了一项新的研究，首次证明了特定遗传因素与抑郁症倾向之间的关系，他开始了对数万名抑郁症患者的雄心勃勃的基因研究，以在分子水平上了解疾病的起源。2017年，加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)成为第一所通过米歇尔·克拉斯克(Michelle Craske)教授开发的STAND计划向学生提供自愿抑郁症和焦虑筛查和即时治疗的大学。今年，美国大萧条大挑战创建了一个免费的新冠肺炎护理套餐，并免费提供给公众，以缓解与大流行相关的压力和焦虑。
这些努力，包括本周开始的研究，形成了一种全面的、多学科的方法来理解抑郁症，开发治疗方法，并抹去颠覆生活的耻辱。
弗莱默说：“这项研究的规模、长度和设计使得分析成为可能，这将提供迄今为止关于可能使用数字工具评估和跟踪行为健康的最广泛的证据。”“我们设想，在未来，这些工具将成为抑郁症患者和那些为他们提供护理的人不可或缺的工具。”
note_en:
While the capability to diagnose cancer and heart problems has advanced by giant steps in recent years, methods to detect depression have stubbornly stayed the same for more than a century: Observe patients, and ask them how they are doing.
  UCLA has launched a major new study, sponsored by and in collaboration with Apple, designed to help revolutionize detection and treatment of depression.
  The three-year study, which begins this week, was co-designed by researchers at UCLA and Apple to obtain objective measures of factors such as sleep, physical activity, heart rate and daily routines to illuminate the relationship between these factors and symptoms of depression and anxiety.
  The research will utilize Apple technology including iPhone, Apple Watch and a Beddit sleep-monitoring device. Making the connection between quantifiable data and symptoms of anxiety and depression could enable health care providers to note warning signs and prevent the onset of depressive episodes, track the effectiveness of treatment and identify causes of depression.
  “As a neuroscientist by training with expertise in sleep, I am incredibly excited about this collaboration and am hopeful that it will lead to significant strides in mental health research,” said UCLA Chancellor Gene Block.
  Dr. Nelson Freimer, distinguished professor of psychiatry and director of the UCLA Depression Grand Challenge, is principal investigator on the study.
  “This collaboration, which harnesses UCLA’s deep research expertise and Apple’s innovative technology, has the potential to transform behavioral health research and clinical care,” Freimer said. “Current approaches to treating depression rely almost entirely on the subjective recollections of depression sufferers. This is an important step for obtaining objective and precise measurements that guide both diagnosis and treatment.”
  The study is the latest milestone for the  Depression Grand Challenge, an ambitious UCLA initiative involving researchers from across disciplines to identify genetic and environmental factors that contribute to depression, understand the biological changes that depression causes in the brain and body, accelerate progress in diagnosis and treatment and end the stigma associated with the disorder. UCLA chose to take on this challenge because depression afflicts more than 300 million people worldwide, resulting in nearly 1 million suicides a year.
  The pilot phase of the study, involving 150 participants recruited from among UCLA Health patients, begins this week. The main phases, to take place from 2021 through 2023, will involve some 3,000 participants, drawn both from UCLA Health patients and the UCLA student body.
  Participants will need to download a research app on their personal iPhones. They will receive an Apple Watch and Beddit sleep monitor, which they will use for the duration of the study. Participants will share relevant information through periodic clinical interviews and questionnaires, as well as from data obtained from the phone, watch and sleep monitor.
  Freimer emphasizes that ensuring the privacy and security of study participants’ data is a high priority for both UCLA and Apple. UCLA will process and maintain study data in a secure environment, with access limited to members of the UCLA research team. UCLA and Apple will analyze the data only after they are coded and stripped of names and other contact information.
  The study comes as the COVID-19 pandemic has disrupted lives and spurred a focus on anxiety and depression, and when physical distancing requirements have made scientific research challenging.
  “UCLA and Apple have designed this study so that all aspects of participation can be accomplished remotely,” Freimer said. “The pandemic has heightened anxiety and depression globally, and has increased awareness of the importance of behavioral health to overall wellbeing. At the same time, physical distancing requirements have limited in-person mental health assessment and treatment, leading to expanded use and acceptance of telehealth. These changes highlight the importance of incorporating technologies like those to be tested in this study into clinical research and eventually into practice.”
  The UCLA Depression Grand Challenge has already made significant advances in understanding and treating depression. Professor Jonathan Flint, who led a  study proving for the first time the relationship between specific genetic factors and a tendency toward depression, has embarked on ambitious genetic research of tens of thousands of depression sufferers to understand the origins of the disease at the molecular level. In 2017, UCLA became the first university to  offer voluntary depression and anxiety screening and immediate treatment to students through the STAND program developed by Professor Michelle Craske. This year, the  Depression Grand Challenge created a  COVID-19 Care Package and to offered it for free to members of the public to ease stress and anxiety associated with the pandemic.
  These efforts, including the study that starts this week, form a comprehensive, multi-disciplinary approach to understanding depression, developing treatments and wiping away the stigma that has upended lives.
  “The analyses made possible by the scale, length and design of this study will provide the most extensive evidence available to date regarding the possible uses of digital tools for assessing and tracking behavioral health,” Freimer said. “We envision a future in which these tools will become indispensable for depression sufferers and those providing them care.”
title: 加州大学洛杉矶分校和苹果公司发起了一项研究，看看iPhone、手表和Beddit睡眠监控器是否有助于了解日常生活是如何影响抑郁症状的

三星明天的Unpack发布会将是谣言四起、泄露甚多的Galaxy Note 20的官方展示会。对于那些想在手机揭幕前再看一遍这款手机的人来说，AT&amp；的一段宣传视频几乎展示了这款手机的每一寸地方，甚至详细介绍了它最重要的规格和新功能。
这段视频是由南美科技博主卡洛斯·秘鲁(Karlos秘鲁)在Twitter上弹出的，似乎是为了宣传这款新手机及其更大的Ultra变种而准备的营销材料。它没有告诉我们任何我们之前不知道或怀疑三星即将推出的超大手机的突破性信息，但在正式发布之前，一个包含如此多信息和细节的视频出现在屏幕上，这是相当令人震惊的。
Gracias a la Operadora AT&amp；T Podemos over un video Promocional de Los Próximos Galaxy Note 20 Series Anes del del#Unpack Prácticamente confirmado todas las filtraciones y con algunos delalles que no gustaran a mohos.。。#SamsungUnpack#GalaxyNote20Series pic.twitter.com/UZZ43RPVJ0。
-Karlos秘鲁(@Karlos秘鲁)2020年8月3日。
当然，视频中详细介绍了这两款手机--标准的Galaxy Note 20和Galaxy Note 20 Ultra，这两款手机都将具备5G功能。它还详细介绍了高通骁龙865 Plus处理器和Ultra版的6.9英寸Infinity-O Dynamic AMOLED 2X显示屏，据称这款显示屏的分辨率为3200 x 1440，可变刷新率高达120 Hz。这段视频甚至详细说明了两款设备屏幕的区别，标准版本是6.7英寸的Super AMOLED Plus显示屏。
这段视频经过了新的S Pen，用于Word和PowerPoint的Microsoft Office触控笔集成，8K视频功能，标准Note 20上的6400万像素摄像头和用于Ultra变体的1.08亿像素摄像头，以及30倍Space Zoom(Ultra是50倍)。如果你想知道电池的大小，这个促销广告里也有。根据视频，标准的Note 20将拥有4300mAh的电池，而Ultra将拥有4500mAh的电池。最后，视频还详细介绍了颜色选项：以神秘绿色、神秘灰色和神秘青铜为标准；以神秘青铜、神秘黑色和神秘白色为标准。
这基本上都与我们明天预期的情况一致。但再说一次，在官方产品发布会之前看到如此广泛的泄密事件仍然有点令人困惑，很明显，目前三星没有合适的方法来遏制这样的事情，除非它从根本上改变活动、营销甚至内部设计和制造的方式。
note_en:
Samsung’s Unpacked event tomorrow will be the official showcase for the heavily rumored and  much-leaked Galaxy Note 20. For those who wanted  yet another look at the phone in full before it’s unveiling, an AT&T promotional video pretty much shows off every inch of the device and even details its most important specs and new features.
 The video, which popped up on Twitter  courtesy of South American tech blogger Karlos Peru, appears to have been prepped as a piece of marketing material to promote the new phone and its larger Ultra variant. It doesn’t tell us anything groundbreaking that we didn’t already know or suspect about Samsung’s upcoming extra-large phones, but it is pretty staggering that a video with this much information and detail is popping up ahead of an official announcement.
 Gracias a la Operadora AT&T podemos ver un video promocional de los próximos Galaxy Note 20 series antes del  #Unpacked   Prácticamente confirmado todas las filtraciones y con algunos detalles que no gustaran a muchos.  . #SamsungUnpacked  #GalaxyNote20Series  pic.twitter.com/UZZ43RPVJ0
— KarlosPeru (@KarlosPeru)  August 3, 2020
 The video details, of course, the two phones — the standard Galaxy Note 20 and the Galaxy Note 20 Ultra, both of which will have 5G capabilities. It also details the Qualcomm Snapdragon 865 Plus processor and the Ultra version’s 6.9-inch Infinity-O Dynamic AMOLED 2X display, which is said to have a 3200 x 1440 resolution with a variable refresh rate of up to 120Hz. The video even details the difference between the two devices’ screens, with the standard version being a 6.7-inch Super AMOLED Plus display.
 The video goes through the new S Pen, a Microsoft Office stylus integration for Word and PowerPoint, 8K video capabilities, the 64-megapixel camera on the standard Note 20 and 108-megapixel one for the Ultra variant, and 30x Space Zoom (50x for the Ultra). In case you were wondering about the battery size, this promotional spot has it, too. According to the video, the standard Note 20 will have a 4,300mAh battery, while the Ultra will have a 4,500mAh one. Finally, the video also details the color options: mystic green, mystic gray, and mystic bronze for the standard; mystic bronze, mystic black, and mystic white for the Ultra.
 This basically all aligns with what we’re expecting to see tomorrow. But again, it’s still always a bit befuddling to see such extensive leaks ahead of an official product event, and it’s pretty clear at this point Samsung has no proper way to contain stuff like this unless it radically changes its approach to events, marketing, and perhaps even internal design and manufacturing.
title: 三星Galaxy Note 20 Ultra几乎所有功能都在AT&T的新视频中泄露
title_en: Samsung Galaxy Note 20 Ultra has almost every feature leaked in new AT&T video

在“社交距离”这个词进入社会用语的一年前，电子商务营销初创公司MikMak的创始人雷切尔·蒂波格(Rachel Tipograph)站在董事会面前做出了一个回想起来似乎再明显不过的预测：电子商务的未来将是在线杂货和酒类购物。
2019年初，杂货和酒类销售仍大多处于线下。根据eMarketer的数据，到第二季度，预计2019年只有2%左右的食品和饮料销售发生在网上，2022年达到3%。在Tipograph看来，有颠覆的空间-那是在新冠肺炎让每个人在家购物之前。
MikMak在2019年剩余的时间里一直在为营销人员利用数字广告表现作为追踪电商销售的一种方式而努力，他在2月份推出了新产品，就在全球各大城市进入封锁的前几周。
这些大流行前的押注现在得到了回报：MikMak现在已经在首轮融资中筹集了1000万美元，以加快其数据分析路线图并进行国际扩张。
“我创造了一个领先于市场的产品，”Tipograph说。“然后在3月9日，市场来了。”
今天宣布的这笔资金由Wavecrest Growth Partners以及包括卢米纳里资本公司和勇敢风险投资公司在内的现有投资者牵头。一些新投资者也参与其中，包括午餐伙伴、BazaarVoice创始人布雷特·赫特、HookLogic创始人兼首席执行官乔纳森·奥普代克、Foursquare首席执行官大卫·希姆和Kargo首席执行官哈里·卡尔格曼。
Tipograph在2014年福布斯30岁以下30岁以下营销和广告排行榜上名列前茅，他在数字广告趋势方面有着领先的记录。当MikMak在2015年推出时，它寻求以30秒视频的形式重新发明数字时代的信息广告。虽然该公司最初专注于帮助营销人员创建和衡量“可购物内容”，但在2017年，它转向企业软件，以帮助大品牌更好地衡量电子商务营销。
Tipograph说：“从很多方面来说，所有事情都是在进行明智的押注。”“自2019年初以来，MikMak下了几个非常聪明的赌注。”
根据Tipograph的数据，该公司自2018年以来一直有正现金流，不需要筹集资金。然而，随着电子商务在大流行期间的蓬勃发展，加快她的路线图似乎是正确的时机。虽然谷歌和Facebook等许多其他在线广告巨头都将电子商务分析的重点放在消费者洞察力上，但MikMak则专注于通过亚马逊、塔吉特、Drizly、沃尔玛、迷你吧和Petco等电子商务网站推动B2B销售。Tipograph表示，MikMak的顶级客户现在通过20多个渠道进行流量，利用该平台将广告支出与消费者需求、库存和销售联系起来。
“当你是一个大众市场品牌时，你会依赖市场和批发商来销售和分销你的产品，”她说。“当谈到电子商务时，即使网上有这么多数据，他们实际上也不会访问这些数据。因此，在过去20年的电子商务增长中，这些庞然大物的品牌仍然对他们的客户一无所知。他们不知道能起作用的内容，不知道描述，不知道广告定向，不知道捆绑。对他们来说，这完全是个黑匣子。“。
自3月初以来，该公司营收增加了50%，同时员工人数翻了一番。在2022年进入亚洲之前，新一轮融资将有助于在欧洲和拉丁美洲的扩张。(MikMak最近还宣布，将开始不分地点招聘员工，并计划在2020年底之前再增加30名员工。)。
事实上，电子商务继续增长。根据Statista的数据，美国的在线零售额今年可能达到3740亿美元，2021年可能达到4070亿美元，而到2024年将达到4760亿美元。Adobe的数据显示，当仅在杂货店购物时，4月份在线杂货店销售额增长了110%，而葡萄酒、啤酒和烈性酒的购买量增长了74%。根据Brick Meets Click和Mercatus的研究，6月份，美国在线食品杂货销售额增长到72亿美元，比一个月前增长了9%。事实上，4560万个家庭使用送货和提货服务，而在线杂货购物的家庭普及率提高到35%。
MikMak并不是唯一一家在疫情期间筹集资金的电商公司。今年5月，总部位于圣地亚哥的Ecwid宣布已筹集4200万美元，帮助小企业开展电子商务努力。就在上个月，总部位于洛杉矶的Scalefast筹集了2200万美元的B系列资金，以帮助各品牌迅速推出在线商店。
最近的电商热潮让品牌更有理由关注消费者需求。Tipograph说，了解受众和他们回应的广告类型是很重要的。当涉及到用高需求的产品获得或保护市场份额时，这一点尤其正确。
“当你是一个大众品牌，而你的客户缺货时，”Tipograph说，“客户对你没有忠诚度。”
note_en:
A year before the phrase “social distancing” entered society’s vernacular, Rachel Tipograph—the founder of the e-commerce marketing startup MikMak—stood in front of her board of directors and made a prediction that in retrospect seems all too obvious: the future of e-commerce would be online grocery and liquor shopping.
  At the beginning of 2019, grocery and alcohol sales were still mostly offline. By the second quarter, only around 2% of food and beverage sales were expected to occur online in 2019 before hitting 3% in 2022, according to eMarketer. From Tipograph’s view, there was room for disruption—and that was before Covid-19 had everyone shopping from home.
  MikMak spent the rest of 2019 building out ways for marketers to use digital ad performance as a way to track e-commerce sales, launching new products in February just weeks before major cities around the world went into lockdown.
    Those pre-pandemic bets are now paying off: MikMak has now raised $10 million in Series A funding to accelerate its data analytics road map and expand internationally.
   “I built a product that was ahead of the market,” Tipograph says. “And then on March 9, the market arrived.”
  The funding, announced today, was led by Wavecrest Growth Partners, as well as existing investors including Luminari Capital and BraVe Ventures. A number of new investors also participated, including Lunch Partners, Bazaarvoice founder Brett Hurt, Hooklogic founder and CEO Jonathan Opdyke, Foursquare CEO David Shim and Kargo CEO Harry Kargman.
    Tipograph—who was featured on the 2014  Forbes 30 Under 30 list in the marketing and advertising category—has a track record of being ahead of digital advertising trends. When MikMak launched in 2015, it sought to  reinvent infomercials for the digital era in the form of 30-second videos. And while the company had initially focused on helping marketers create and measure “shoppable content,” it pivoted to enterprise software in 2017 to help large brands better measure e-commerce marketing.
  “Everything in many ways is placing an educated bet,” Tipograph says. “And MikMak placed a few really smart bets since the beginning of 2019.”
  According to Tipograph, the company has had positive cash flow since 2018 and didn’t need to raise money. However, with e-commerce booming during the pandemic, the timing seemed right to accelerate her road map. And while many other online advertising giants like Google and Facebook have focused their e-commerce analytics on consumer insights, MikMak has focused on driving B2B sales through e-commerce websites such as Amazon, Target, Drizly, Walmart, Minibar and Petco. Tipograph says top MikMak clients now traffic across more than two dozen channels, using the platform to link ad spend to consumer demand, inventory and sales.
  “When you’re a mass-market brand, you rely on marketplaces and wholesalers to sell and distribute your products,” she says. “And when it comes to e-commerce, even though there’s so much data online, they actually don’t access any of that. So over the last 20 years of e-commerce growth, these behemoth brands still knew nothing about their customers. They didn’t know the content that would work, the description, the ad-targeting, the bundling. It was a total black box for them.”
  Since early March, the company has increased revenue by 50% while doubling its headcount. The new round of fundraising will help with expansion in Europe and Latin America before moving into Asia in 2022. (MikMak also recently announced that it would begin hiring employees regardless of location and plans to add another 30 employees by the end of 2020.)
  Indeed, e-commerce continues to grow. According to  Statista, online retail sales in the U.S. could total $374 billion this year and $407 billion in 2021 while reaching $476 billion by 2024. When it comes to only grocery shopping, data from  Adobe shows online grocery sales increased 110% in April, while the purchase of wine, beer and spirits increased 74%. And in June, U.S. online grocery sales grew to $7.2 billion—up 9% from just a month earlier—according to research from  Brick Meets Click and Mercatus. In fact, 45.6 million households use delivery and pickup services, while household penetration for online grocery shopping increased to 35%.
  MikMak isn’t the only e-commerce company to raise money during the pandemic. In May, San Diego-based Ecwid announced it had raised $42 million to help small businesses with e-commerce efforts. And just last month, Los Angeles-based Scalefast raised $22 million in Series B funding to help brands quickly launch online stores.
  The recent e-commerce boom gives brands all the more reason to pay attention to consumer demand. Tipograph says it’s important to understand the audience and the types of ads they respond to. That’s especially true when it comes to gaining or protecting marketshare with products that are in high demand.
  “When you’re a mass brand and your customer is out of stock,” Tipograph says, “the customer has no loyalty to you.”
title: MikMak的SaaS让品牌可以统一在线零售商的数字店面，该公司由Wavecrest Growth Partners牵头筹集了1000万美元的首轮融资
title_en: MikMak, whose SaaS lets brands unify their digital storefront across online retailers, raises $10M Series A led by Wavecrest Growth Partners
